model.name: DREAM S2 news-cycle retention
model.lambda_interpretation: lambda = elapsed hours since topic attention peak
model.signal_preprocessing: Formal S2 fit uses circadian-corrected publish-time article counts: raw count divided by expected global publishing/activity factor.
model.observation_clock: article.published_at, not GitHub Action run time
model.history_role: data/history.json retains raw article records and first_seen/last_seen provenance; publication time reconstructs the curve.
model.comparison: Delta AIC = AIC(exponential beta=1) - AIC(stretched S2 beta free)
model.cycle_archive_role: data/cycles.json stores completed formal cycle summaries so prior S2 learning remains visible when a new wave resets the current topic.

articles.summary: I’ve been building Echo ( https://echo.tracerml.ai/ ), an experiment in making one AI system out of a pool of open-weight models rather than choosing a single model and using it for every task. It started with a simple experiment. I took a group of models, including GLM-5.2, Kimi K2.7 and others, and ran them on the same evaluations. articles.title: Thousands evacuated in south of France as wildfire rages across pine forest – video
articles.summary: Local people and tourists were evacuated from the Arcachon Bay area overnight into Thursday after a huge fire engulfed more than 3,400 hectares (8,400 acres) of forest. Pushed by the wind, with flames reaching 10 metres high, the fire is still spreading towards the Cap Ferret peninsula. On Tuesday, two firefighters died battling another nearby fire close to Bordeaux airport Thousands flee wildfires in France as temperatures reach extreme levels in southern Europe Continue reading...
